projectName: Agentic Graph RAG for Medical Diagnosis
projectDescription: A multi-hop agentic Graph RAG system for medical question answering that combines semantic and relational retrieval channels backed by Neo4j and Milvus.
keyFeatures: Agentic multi-hop reasoning with iterative sub-query decomposition and back-references across hops
Dual retrieval channels: parallel semantic (text) and relational (knowledge graph triple) pipelines
Four switchable Graph RAG backends: LightRAG, MiniRAG, PathRAG, HyperGraphRAG
Neo4j for entity/relationship knowledge graph storage
Milvus for dense hybrid vector search over medical documents
LangGraph orchestration with stateful subgraphs, conditional loop edges, and fan-out/fan-in
Evaluation via DeepEval against HealthBench, MedCaseReasoning, MetaMedQA, and PubMedQA benchmarks
publicApiSurface: LightRAG backend — hybrid dual-level KG retrieval via Neo4j + vector index
MiniRAG backend — lightweight graph retrieval in 'light' mode
PathRAG backend — hierarchical two-tier path traversal in hybrid mode
HyperGraphRAG backend — hyperedge-aware retrieval combining vector search with hypergraph traversal
context_filter — splits raw backend context into semantic and relational channel inputs
LangGraph parent graph — fans out to semantic and relational subgraphs, then synthesizes final answer
Semantic channel subgraph — sub-query grounding → retrieval → filter → summary → logic drafting → evidence verification
Relational channel subgraph — SPO triple queries → KG filter → triplet summaries → sub-answer
DeepEval evaluation harness — Contextual Recall, Contextual Precision, Contextual Relevancy, Answer Relevancy, Faithfulness
